entities.name: Francis Geisdorff
entities.context: Named in patent title page, 1919; Crow Indian allottee

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Crow allottee #2336, deceased; allotted land in 1907; died November 27, 1918 of influenza aged about 20

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Sr.
entities.context: Father of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; received leasing papers from Superintendent Asbury in August 1918

entities.name: Emily J. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; lived at Randlett/Fort Duchesne, Utah; heir to estate

entities.name: Emily M. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily M.; care of Uintah & Ouray Agency

entities.name: Emily F. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily F. in May 1919 letter

entities.name: Emily Lucile Geisdorff
entities.context: Minor daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff; born May 23, 1918; heir to estate

entities.name: Thomas R. Powers
entities.context: Purchaser of Geisdorff allotment land; of Wyola, Montana; also referenced as residing in Hartman, Wyoming

entities.name: C. H. Asbury
entities.context: Superintendent at Crow Agency, Montana; signed multiple letters regarding the Geisdorff land sale

entities.name: Albert H. Kneale
entities.context: Superintendent at Uintah and Ouray Agency, Fort Duchesne, Utah; assisted with Geisdorff sale matters

entities.name: Calvin Coolidge
entities.context: President of the United States; signed trust patent for Francis Geisdorff on December 21, 1923

entities.name: Woodrow Wilson
entities.context: President of the United States; signed fee patent No. 719146 on November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: John McPhaul
entities.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office; signed statement October 13, 1919 approving fee patent issuance

entities.name: Eloise Whitebear Pease
entities.context: Named on collection stamps throughout document; collector/owner of these historical documents

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Indians
entities.context: Tribal entity; land argued to revert to tribe upon allottee death under trust patent terms

entities.name: Crow Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency in Montana administering Crow Reservation land matters

entities.name: Uintah and Ouray Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency at Fort Duchesne, Utah; jurisdiction over Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow

entities.name: Office of Indian Affairs
entities.context: Federal bureau overseeing Indian land sales and allotments; Washington D.C.

entities.name: General Land Office
entities.context: Federal office issuing patents; located in Washington D.C.; issued fee patent No. 719146

entities.name: Department of the Interior
entities.context: Federal department overseeing Indian affairs and land patents

entities.context: Bank holding $7.98 lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate

entities.name: Sheridan Banking Company
entities.context: Bank on which Thomas R. Powers drew checks dated November 2, 1918 for land purchase

entities.context: Location of Uintah and Ouray Agency; residence of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow

entities.context: Residence of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. at time of allotment sale proceedings

entities.context: Address of Thomas R. Powers, purchaser of Geisdorff allotment

entities.context: Address of Thomas R. Powers in December 1918 correspondence

entities.name: Billings, Montana

entities.name: 159.67 acres - Geisdorff allotment
entities.context: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment; S½NE¼, SE¼NW¼, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S, R.34E, Montana

entities.name: 648.04 acres - Francis Geisdorff allotment
entities.context: Lots 9,10,11,12 of Section 2 and N½SW¼, N½SE¼, N½S½SE¼ of Section 11, T.1S, R.34E, Montana; 648.04 acres

entities.name: Geisdorff family allotments
entities.context: Multiple allotments belonging to siblings and relatives of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; several hundred acres combined grazing land

events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S½NE¼, SE¼NW¼, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S, R.34E) as Crow allottee #2336; trust patent issued under Act of February 8, 1887
Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent No. 227584 issued by President Calvin Coolidge
Petition for Sale of Land by original allottee Frederick Geisdorff Jr. received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made offer to lease Geisdorff allotment land; appraisal made for lease request
Crow Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment for sale on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale; applicant's age of under 21 overlooked
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned acceptance form to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 signed written acceptance of his bid of $1,500 for 159.67 acres; balance of purchase price demanded
Superintendent acknowledged receipt of two checks from Thomas R. Powers for $1,350 and $150 (balance of sale price); letter written to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. about age concern
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza aged about 20 years; survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency notifying of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s death from influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers about age issue and offer to cancel sale and return payment
Emily Lucile Geisdorff born; daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff
Uintah & Ouray Agency notified Supt. C. Asbury of Crow Agency of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote Crow Agency Superintendent inquiring about aid in paying note owed by her deceased husband
Superintendent answered Mrs. Geisdorff's letter; no funds available; mentioned pending sale uncertainty due to allottee's age; promised heirship hearing
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters of land sale; holding two checks from Powers totaling $1,500
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner; asked if she wanted pending sale completed
Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter; stated nothing shows heirs want sale; if heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily F. Geisdorff asking whether she wished sale completed to Powers for $1,500 or bid rejected
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner; stated he had received acceptance of widow of allottee
General Land Office stated no reservations or withdrawals covering land; no reasons why fee patent should not be issued to purchaser; signed by John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs requesting completion of Land Sale No. 991; noted Powers requesting return of deposit if no action taken
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers enclosing copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
Fee Patent No. 719146 issued by President Woodrow Wilson to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres of Geisdorff allotment land
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent No. 719146 to Crow Agency for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signed receipt
Receipt for patent issued; C.H. Asbury acknowledged patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers from Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
Heirship hearing held; testimony showed allottee died November 27, 1918 aged about 20, married, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Report on heirship and recommendations transmitted to Secretary of Interior for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate; heirs determined as wife and daughter under Utah statutes
Check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff as her share; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee from total of $1,521.24

financial_transactions.payee: Crow Agency / Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate
financial_transactions.description: Accepted bid for 159.67 acres of Geisdorff allotment under Crow Land Sale No. 991
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Geisdorff allotment land
Balance of purchase price; check drawn on Sheridan Banking Company dated November 2, 1918
Advertising fee for land sale; check drawn on Sheridan Banking Company; purchase price over $1,000 and under $2,000
Heirship fee authorized by Indian Office; deducted from estate total of $1,521.24
Check No. 10613; Emily J. Geisdorff's share of estate (one half plus dower interest) from sale proceeds
Minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff's share of estate from sale proceeds; held under supervision
Bank account balance derived from lease money deposited to credit of estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Total amount on books at Crow Agency to credit of Frederick Geisdorff estate before deductions
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment (159.67 acres); price per acre $9.39 approximate

relationships.context: Under jurisdiction of Uintah and Ouray school at time of sale proceedings

relationships.context: Administered land sale and heirship proceedings on behalf of allottee and heirs

relationships.subject: Albert H. Kneale
relationships.context: Assisted with sale petition acknowledgment; notified Crow Agency of allottee's death

relationships.context: Indian of the Crow Reservation; received homestead allotment 1923

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for Geisdorff allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Answer regarding leasing of allotment land
correspondence.outcome: Unknown from records

correspondence.subject: Age concern regarding sale of allotment; request for birth date and relatives' names
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names/addresses of all relatives; obtain statement from Superintendent Kneale recommending sale
correspondence.outcome: No reply received from allottee before his death

correspondence.subject: Acceptance of bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991; request for balance of purchase price
correspondence.action_requested: Remit balance due on purchase price within 30 days; sign affidavit of vendee in duplicate
correspondence.outcome: Powers paid balance; checks received November 5, 1918

correspondence.subject: Age issue with Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; possible cancellation of sale
correspondence.action_requested: Offered to cancel sale and return payment upon request
correspondence.outcome: Sale ultimately proceeded after heirship determination

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None; informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death; sale proceedings suspended pending heirship

correspondence.subject: Request for aid in paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance to pay husband's debt
correspondence.outcome: Denied; no funds available

correspondence.subject: Response to request for financial aid; pending sale status; heirship hearing notice
correspondence.action_requested: Informed widow of pending sale uncertainty and upcoming heirship hearing
correspondence.outcome: Widow notified; heirship hearing subsequently held

correspondence.subject: Review of all matters concerning Geisdorff land sale; holding checks from Powers totaling $1,500
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale; approval of sale
correspondence.outcome: Washington responded April 26, 1919 stating heirs must want sale to proceed

correspondence.subject: Pending sale of allotment; inquiry whether widow wants sale completed
correspondence.action_requested: Widow to indicate whether she wants pending sale completed
correspondence.outcome: Widow eventually provided acceptance

correspondence.sender: Chief Clerk, Office of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Response to March 24 letter; death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; sale status
correspondence.action_requested: If probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers; otherwise reject bid and readvertise after heirs determined
correspondence.outcome: Asbury proceeded to obtain widow's acceptance and forward sale papers

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of allotment of deceased husband; inquiry whether to complete sale to Powers for $1,500 or reject bid
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority if she wants sale to proceed; write if she does not
correspondence.outcome: Widow signed acceptance; sale proceeded

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991; enclosure of sale papers; widow's acceptance received
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991; Powers requesting return of deposit if no action taken
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at early date
correspondence.outcome: General Land Office cleared patent October 13, 1919; patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Geisdorff sale; copy of letter to Indian Bureau
correspondence.action_requested: Informational
correspondence.outcome: Patent subsequently issued

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office
correspondence.subject: Clearance for fee patent issuance to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: None; clearance statement
correspondence.outcome: Fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent No. 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to person entitled; forward original receipt; retain duplicate
correspondence.outcome: Patent delivered; receipt signed December 19, 1919

correspondence.subject: Proceeds of sale; transfer to agency; minor's share
correspondence.action_requested: Widow to request transfer of proceeds to her agency after heirs determined; minor's shares require special authority
correspondence.outcome: Heirs determined; payment made April 27, 1920

correspondence.subject: Fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: Informational notification
correspondence.outcome: Patent recorded

correspondence.subject: Heirship to estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration of heirship report and recommendations
correspondence.outcome: Heirs determined; payments made April 27, 1920

correspondence.subject: Distribution of estate proceeds; check No. 10613 for $1,317.26
correspondence.action_requested: Receipt of payment
correspondence.outcome: Payment distributed; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily

correspondence.subject: Birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff on May 23, 1918
correspondence.action_requested: Informational
correspondence.outcome: Emily Lucile recognized as heir in heirship proceedings

legislative_actions.outcome: Dawes Act; trust patent for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment issued December 2, 1907 under this act
Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr. was allotted 159.67 acres on Crow Reservation
Act under which Francis Geisdorff was allotted 648.04 acres as homestead on Crow Reservation; trust patent No. 227584 issued December 21, 1923
Referenced in fee patent No. 719146 approval; related to water rights and reclamation provisions
Referenced as basis for Crow Tribe land ownership claim; analyst notes land was first and foremost property of Crow Tribe under this treaty
